title_en: Ibn Hajar al-Haytami: Life and Scholarly Formation
title_ar: ابن حجر الهيتمي: حياته وتكوينه العلمي
content_en:
Shihab ad-Din Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Hajar al-Haytami was born in 909 AH (1504 CE) in Abu Haytam, a village in the Nile Delta of Egypt. He studied in Cairo at al-Azhar under some of the most eminent scholars of his era, including Zakariyya al-Ansari, who was also the teacher of ar-Ramli — a circumstance that makes the later divergence between Ibn Hajar and ar-Ramli particularly interesting, since they were, in a sense, scholarly siblings from the same master. Ibn Hajar's education was deep and broad, encompassing the full range of Islamic sciences.

Around 948 AH (1541 CE), Ibn Hajar left Egypt and settled in Mecca, where he spent the remainder of his long life. He taught in the sacred mosque, wrote prolifically, and issued fatwas that were consulted across the Islamic world. His physical presence in the Hijaz, combined with the prestige of Mecca as the center of Muslim pilgrimage, gave his scholarly authority a unique reach: pilgrims who came from Indonesia, India, East Africa, and elsewhere would attend his lessons, carry his books home, and disseminate his legal positions across the Shafi'i world. This is the primary reason why his positions came to dominate the Shafi'i tradition in Southeast Asia and East Africa.

His scholarly output is vast. Beyond Tuhfat al-Muhtaj, he wrote al-Zawajir an Iqtiraf al-Kaba'ir (a treatise on major sins), Fath al-Jawwad (a commentary on Irshad al-Ghawi by Ibn al-Muqri), and numerous other works in jurisprudence, theology, and hadith. His fatwa collections preserve detailed responses to the most contested legal questions of his era. He died in Mecca in 974 AH (1567 CE), having shaped the Shafi'i tradition for generations. His long residence at the heart of the Islamic world, combined with his prolific output and the wide circulation of his works through pilgrimage networks, gave his scholarship a geographic reach that few scholars based in a single city have equaled. The result was a form of scholarly authority that transcended regional Shafi'i sub-traditions, making Tuhfat al-Muhtaj a reference consulted as readily in Hadhramaut and Sumatra as in Cairo and Damascus.
title_id: Ibn Hajar al-Haytami: Kehidupan dan Pembentukan Keilmuan
content_id:
Syihab ad-Din Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Hajar al-Haytami lahir pada 909 H (1504 M) di Abu Haytam, sebuah desa di Delta Nil Mesir. Ia menuntut ilmu di Kairo di al-Azhar di bawah bimbingan beberapa ulama paling terkemuka di eranya, termasuk Zakariyya al-Anshari, yang juga merupakan guru ar-Ramli — sebuah keadaan yang membuat divergensi kemudian antara Ibn Hajar dan ar-Ramli menjadi sangat menarik, karena mereka, dalam arti tertentu, adalah saudara keilmuan dari master yang sama. Pendidikan Ibn Hajar sangat mendalam dan luas, mencakup seluruh spektrum ilmu-ilmu Islam.

Sekitar 948 H (1541 M), Ibn Hajar meninggalkan Mesir dan menetap di Mekah, di mana ia menghabiskan sisa hidupnya yang panjang. Ia mengajar di Masjidil Haram, menulis secara produktif, dan mengeluarkan fatwa-fatwa yang dikonsultasikan di seluruh dunia Islam. Kehadirannya secara fisik di Hijaz, dikombinasikan dengan prestise Mekah sebagai pusat ibadah haji Muslim, memberi otoritas keilmuannya jangkauan yang unik: para jamaah haji yang datang dari Indonesia, India, Afrika Timur, dan tempat-tempat lain akan menghadiri pelajaran-pelajarannya, membawa kitab-kitabnya pulang, dan menyebarkan posisi-posisi hukumnya di seluruh dunia Syafi'i. Inilah alasan utama mengapa posisi-posisinya mendominasi tradisi Syafi'i di Asia Tenggara dan Afrika Timur.

Output keilmuannya sangat luas. Selain Tuhfat al-Muhtaj, ia menulis al-Zawajir 'an Iqtiraf al-Kaba'ir (risalah tentang dosa-dosa besar), Fath al-Jawwad (komentar atas Irsyad al-Ghawi karya Ibn al-Muqri), dan banyak karya lainnya dalam fikih, teologi, dan hadis. Koleksi-koleksi fatwanya menyimpan respons-respons rinci terhadap pertanyaan-pertanyaan hukum yang paling diperdebatkan di eranya. Ia wafat di Mekah pada 974 H (1567 M), setelah membentuk tradisi Syafi'i selama generasi-generasi. Tinggalnya yang lama di jantung dunia Islam, dikombinasikan dengan output produktifnya dan peredaran luas karya-karyanya melalui jaringan haji, memberi keilmuannya jangkauan geografis yang jarang ditandingi oleh ulama yang berbasis di satu kota saja. Hasilnya adalah sebuah bentuk otoritas keilmuan yang melampaui sub-tradisi Syafi'i regional, menjadikan Tuhfat al-Muhtaj sebuah referensi yang dikonsultasikan sama mudahnya di Hadhramaut dan Sumatra seperti di Kairo dan Damaskus.
